verdict_reason: AC1 and AC2 are both covered with API-seeded deterministic data plus sort-preservation and free-text cases; gaps are pagination-with-search and some regex/assertion hygiene

todos.issue: Three tests build URL assertions with new RegExp and referenceNumber.replace(/-/g, '\\-'); escaping a hyphen outside a character class is a no-op, so the escape is cargo-cult and the regexes are hard to read
todos.fix: Assert with a predicate — await expect(pages.page).toHaveURL((url) => url.searchParams.get('referenceNumber') === created.referenceNumber) — which is exact and needs no escaping

todos.issue: The AC1 test asserts resultsLabel is exactly 'Showing 1 Results', locking in ungrammatical copy as expected behaviour and coupling the search feature's test to the pre-existing label wording
todos.fix: Assert the card count and reference (already done) and relax the label assertion to /1/ or raise a separate ticket for the 'Showing 1 Results' wording

todos.issue: The beforeEach already navigates to the dashboard, then two tests call journey.toNotificationDashboard() again after seeding — the extra navigation doubles the page load for no assertion benefit
todos.fix: Move the seeding into the tests that need it and drop the redundant second navigation, or drop the shared beforeEach for the seeding tests

todos.issue: 'preserves referenceNumber in the URL when a page param is present' only exercises the no-match case, so it never checks that the frontend's pagination links actually carry referenceNumber — which is the behaviour the buildPaginationLinks change added and where the backend's page>1 defect surfaces
todos.fix: Add a case that searches, lands on a result set with more than one page (or asserts pagination is absent for a single match) and follows the next-page link, asserting referenceNumber survives
